taxonomy: Phellopterus littoralis belongs to the family Fabaceae and is a species of flowering plant. The genus Phellopterus is a small group of legumes that are native to Africa. As a member of the Fabaceae family, Phellopterus littoralis is closely related to other legumes such as beans and peas. The plant is further classified into the subfamily Faboideae, which is one of the largest subfamilies of legumes. Its classification into these different levels reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants.

Its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties also make it useful for treating skin conditions and wounds. In some cultures, Phellopterus littoralis is also used to treat digestive issues, such as diarrhea and constipation.
